title: gilia multicaulis var. peduncularis
intro: Gilia multicaulis var. peduncularis, also known as the glandular gilia, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and fever. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits including the reduction of inflammation and the promotion of immune system function. Gilia multicaulis var. peduncularis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal supplements, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
